# MoviPlayer

> Free, open-source online video player that plays MKV, HEVC (H.265), AV1, VP9, 4K and HDR video directly in the browser — no install, no plugins, no server-side transcoding. Every file is decoded locally on the user's device using the browser's WebCodecs API plus an FFmpeg WebAssembly demuxer, so videos are never uploaded.

MoviPlayer exists to play the files browsers normally refuse: HEVC that won't open in Chrome or Firefox, MKV containers the native `<video>` tag ignores, AV1/VP9 streams, and 10-bit 4K HDR movies. It runs anywhere a modern browser runs — including locked-down or managed work laptops where installing VLC is not allowed. It is also published as an npm package (`movi-player`) and a verified Chrome extension.

## Key facts

- Plays in the browser: MKV, MP4, WebM, AVI, MOV, FLV, WMV, MPEG-TS, M4V containers; H.264, H.265/HEVC, AV1, VP8, VP9 video; AAC, Opus, FLAC, MP3, AC3 audio; HDR10 and HLG.
- 100% client-side: nothing is uploaded, stored, or transcoded on a server. Decoding happens locally via WebCodecs, with a software fallback through FFmpeg compiled to WebAssembly.
- No install required: works on managed/work laptops and Chromebooks where desktop players like VLC are blocked.
- Free and open source (Apache-2.0). No ads, no sign-up, no file-size limits.
- Features: external + embedded subtitles (SRT, ASS, VTT), multiple audio tracks, picture-in-picture, playback speed, keyboard shortcuts, thumbnail scrubbing, folder-as-playlist.

## Links

- [Live app](https://moviplayer.com/): open a local file or paste a video URL to play instantly in the browser.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/mrujjwalg/movi-player): source code, issues, and releases.
- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/movi-player): install `movi-player` to embed the `<movi-player>` element or player API in your own site.
- [Documentation](https://mrujjwalg.github.io/movi-player/): API reference, embedding, and the `<movi-player>` web component.
- [Chrome extension](https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/movi-player/ckleeigcopjnpehkjokijokjegknfgej): adds a "Play here" button to any video link.
- [VS Code extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=mrujjwalg.movi-player-vscode): play and preview video files directly inside Visual Studio Code.
- [Compare to native &lt;video&gt;](https://moviplayer.com/compare): side-by-side test of MoviPlayer against the browser's built-in player.

## Common questions

- Can browsers play HEVC? Most cannot natively — HEVC/H.265 is licensed — but MoviPlayer decodes it in the browser via WebCodecs.
- How to play MKV without VLC? Open the .mkv in MoviPlayer at moviplayer.com; no VLC or other software is needed.
- Does it upload my files? No. All decoding is local; files never leave the device.
- Which browsers are supported? Any modern browser with WebCodecs support (Chrome, Edge, Safari, Firefox).
